---
title: "Persuasion via Man and Machine"
subtitle: "A Living Review"
description: "A living review of the science and engineering of persuasion — from evolutionary biology and costly signalling to large language models. Covers computational persuasion, NLP benchmarks, attitude change, and AI-generated influence at scale."
author:
  - name: Yaman Kumar Singla
    email: dr.yamankumar@gmail.com
date: last-modified
---

::: {.living-badge}
Living Review · Draft
:::

This is a **living review** — entries are continuously added and revised as the field evolves.
The LaTeX source, bibliography, and compiled PDF are always available on [GitHub](https://github.com/yamanksingla/yamanksingla.github.io).
Readers are encouraged to [open an issue](https://github.com/yamanksingla/yamanksingla.github.io/issues) to suggest missing work. See also [Behavior in the Wild](https://behavior-in-the-wild.github.io/), a collection of closely related work exploring persuasion and human behavior in real-world settings outside controlled laboratory environments.
---

## Abstract

This living review synthesises the science and engineering of persuasion across
five millennia of scholarship and across the disciplinary boundaries that have
kept the field fragmented — rhetoric, psychology, linguistics, political science,
marketing, and artificial intelligence. We argue that persuasion is best understood
as the optimisation of the effect of a message on a receiver, and that machine
learning is now a fully-fledged participant in that optimisation — as an instrument,
as an object of study, and as a threat.

Because this is a living review, entries are added and revised continuously.
Readers are encouraged to [open an issue](https://github.com/yamanksingla/yamanksingla.github.io/issues)
on GitHub to suggest missing work.

---

## How to Cite

```
@misc{singla2025persuasion,
  author    = {Singla, Yaman Kumar},
  title     = {Persuasion via Man and Machine: A Living Review},
  year      = {2025},
  url       = {https://yamanksingla.github.io},
  note      = {Living review, continuously updated}
}
```
